<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead">
{"type":"task_assignment","taskId":"5","subject":"Implement display + config/shell/hook/access command stubs + flag expansion","description":"In an isolated worktree, implement all stubbed display, config, shell, hook, and access commands and expand flags on existing ones in src/cmd/cmd.zig.\n\nSTUBBED COMMANDS TO IMPLEMENT:\n- choose-client: interactive client chooser, parse -N/-r/-Z/-F/-f/-K/-O/-t, template\n- confirm-before: confirmation prompt, parse -b/-y/-c/-p/-t, command\n- customize-mode: options customization tree UI, parse -N/-Z/-F/-f/-t\n- display-menu: popup menu, parse -O/-M/-b/-c/-C/-H/-s/-S/-t/-T/-x/-y, name/key/command triples\n- display-popup: popup window, parse -B/-C/-E/-k/-N/-b/-c/-d/-e/-h/-s/-S/-t/-T/-w/-x/-y, shell-command\n- set-hook: set/unset hooks, parse -a/-g/-p/-R/-u/-w/-t, hook name + command\n- show-hooks: display hooks, parse -g/-p/-w/-t, hook name filter\n- clear-prompt-history: clear prompt history, parse -T\n- show-prompt-history: show prompt history, parse -T\n- wait-for: event signaling, parse -L/-S/-U, channel name\n- server-access: manage server ACL, parse -a/-d/-l/-r/-w/-t, user\n\nFLAG EXPANSION ON EXISTING:\n- clock-mode: add -t\n- display-message: add -a/-C/-I/-l/-N/-p/-v/-c/-d/-F/-t\n- command-prompt: add -1/-b/-e/-F/-i/-k/-l/-N/-I/-p/-t/-T, template\n- copy-mode: add -d/-e/-H/-M/-q/-S/-u/-s/-t\n- source-file: add -F/-n/-q/-v/-t, glob support\n- if-shell: add -b/-F\n- run-shell: add -b/-C/-E/-c/-d/-t\n- set-option: add -a/-F/-o/-p/-q/-s/-u/-U/-w, expand recognized options\n- show-options: add -A/-g/-H/-p/-q/-s/-v/-w\n- set-environment: implement fully, parse -F/-h/-g/-r/-u/-t\n- show-environment: implement fully, parse -h/-g/-s/-t\n\nAlso create src/cmd/display_commands_test.zig, src/cmd/option_commands_test.zig, and src/cmd/config_shell_test.zig.\n\nReference: tmux(1) 3.5a man page.","assignedBy":"team-lead","timestamp":"2026-04-03T21:08:24.199Z"}
</teammate-message>

<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead">
You are a TEAM WORKER in team "tmux-parity". Your name is "worker-display".
You report to the team lead ("team-lead").

Your task: Implement all stubbed display, config, shell, hook, option, environment, and access commands and expand flags on existing ones in src/cmd/cmd.zig for full tmux 3.5a parity.

STUBBED COMMANDS TO IMPLEMENT:
- choose-client: interactive client chooser UI. Parse -N/-r/-Z/-F/-f/-K/-O/-t, template. Build a client list similar to choose-tree.
- confirm-before: show confirmation prompt before running command. Parse -b (beep), -y (default yes), -c (confirm-key), -p (prompt text), -t (target-client), command to run if confirmed. Set prompt state on pane with the confirmation.
- customize-mode: options customization tree UI. Parse -N/-Z/-F/-f/-t. Show editable options tree similar to choose-tree.
- display-menu: popup menu with name/key/command entries. Parse -O/-M/-b/-c/-C/-H/-s/-S/-t/-T/-x/-y, name/key/command triples. Minimal implementation: display menu items as output text. Full popup rendering can come later.
- display-popup: popup window running a command. Parse -B/-C/-E/-k/-N/-b/-c/-d/-e/-h/-s/-S/-t/-T/-w/-x/-y, shell-command. Minimal implementation: run the shell command directly (like run-shell). Full popup rendering later.
- set-hook: set/unset hooks on events. Parse -a (append), -g (global), -p (pane), -R (run immediately), -u (unset), -w (window), -t (target), hook-name, command. Use the existing hooks registry in src/hooks/hooks.zig.
- show-hooks: display set hooks. Parse -g/-p/-w/-t, hook name filter. Read from hooks registry.
- clear-prompt-history: clear stored prompt history. Parse -T (prompt-type). Add prompt history storage to Server if needed (simple array).
- show-prompt-history: show stored prompt history. Parse -T (prompt-type).
- wait-for: event signaling between commands. Parse -L (lock channel), -S (signal/wake channel), -U (unlock channel), channel name. Add a WaitChannel HashMap to Server struct.
- server-access: manage server access control. Parse -a (allow), -d (deny), -l (list), -r (read-only), -w (write), -t (target), user. Minimal: store ACL entries on Server.

FLAG EXPANSION ON EXISTING:
- cmdClockMode: add -t (target-pane)
- cmdDisplayMessage: add -a (all clients), -C (set client size), -I (list server info), -l (log), -N (dismiss popup), -p (stdout), -v (verbose), -c (client), -d (delay), -F (format), -t (target)
- cmdCommandPrompt: add -1/-b/-e/-F/-i/-k/-l/-N/-I/-p/-t/-T, template
- cmdCopyMode: add -d (cancel), -e (exit on scroll bottom), -H (hide position), -M (mouse), -q (cancel if already in), -S (scroll), -u (scroll up), -s (src-pane), -t (target-pane)
- cmdSourceFile: add -F (format expand path), -n (syntax check only), -q (quiet), -v (verbose), -t (target). Support glob patterns in path.
- cmdIfShell: add -b (background/async), -F (format check without executing)
- cmdRunShell: add -b (background), -C (tmux command instead of shell), -E (update env), -c (start-dir), -d (delay), -t (target-pane)
- cmdSetOption: add -a (append), -F (format expand), -o (only if unset), -p (pane scope), -q (quiet), -s (server scope), -u (unset), -U (unset and inherit), -w (window scope). Expand recognized option names.
- cmdShowOptions: add -A (include inherited), -g (global), -H (hooks), -p (pane), -q (quiet), -s (server), -v (value only), -w (window). Show all recognized options.
- cmdSetEnvironment: implement fully with -F (format), -h (hidden), -g (global), -r (remove), -u (unset), -t (target-session)
- cmdShowEnvironment: implement fully with -h (hidden), -g (global), -s (shell format), -t (target-session)

Also create:
- src/cmd/display_commands_test.zig - tests for display/interactive commands
- src/cmd/option_commands_test.zig - tests for option/environment commands
- src/cmd/config_shell_test.zig - tests for config/shell/hook/access commands

IMPORTANT: Register all three test files in src/main.zig.

Follow existing patterns in cmd.zig. For hooks integration, use the existing hooks.zig types.
For wait-for channels, add a simple HashMap([]const u8, std.ArrayListAligned(std.c.fd_t, null)) to Server.
After implementation, run: zig build test
Fix any compilation or test failures before reporting completion.

When done, report to team-lead via SendMessage.
</teammate-message>

file.content:
const signals = @import("signals.zig");

/// Server state.
pub const Server = struct {
    listen_fd: std.c.fd_t,
    socket_path: []const u8,
    sessions: std.ArrayListAligned(*Session, null),
    clients: std.ArrayListAligned(ClientConnection, null),
    default_session: ?*Session,
    choose_tree_state: ?ChooseTreeState,
    paste_stack: PasteStack,
    session_loop: SessionLoop,
    binding_manager: binding_mod.BindingManager,
    global_default_shell: ?[:0]u8,
    config_file: ?[]const u8,
    messages: std.ArrayListAligned([]u8, null),
    running: bool,
    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,

    pub const ClientConnection = struct {
        fd: std.c.fd_t,
        session: ?*Session,
        identified: bool,
        choose_tree_state: ?ChooseTreeState,
        locked: bool = false,
        pid: i32 = 0,
    };

    pub fn init(alloc: std.mem.Allocator, socket_path: []const u8) !Server {
        var bm = binding_mod.BindingManager.init(alloc);
        try bm.setupDefaults();

        return .{
            .listen_fd = -1,
            .socket_path = try alloc.dupe(u8, socket_path),
            .sessions = .empty,
            .clients = .empty,
            .default_session = null,
            .choose_tree_state = null,
            .paste_stack = PasteStack.init(alloc),
            .session_loop = SessionLoop.init(alloc),
            .binding_manager = bm,
            .global_default_shell = null,
            .config_file = null,
            .messages = .empty,
            .running = false,
            .allocator = alloc,
        };
    }

content.text: The `zig fmt` hook runs after every Read, causing the staleness. I'll use a targeted Python script to batch all changes atomically:

input.command:
python3 - <<'PYEOF'
import re

path = "/Users/zchee/src/github.com/zchee/agentmux/src/cmd/cmd.zig"
with open(path, 'r') as f:
    src = f.read()

replacements = [
    # registerBuiltins handler wiring
    ('set-hook", .alias = null, .min_args = 0, .max_args = 10, .usage = "set-hook [-agpRuw] [-t target-pane] hook [command]", .handler = cmdNotImplemented',
     'set-hook", .alias = null, .min_args = 0, .max_args = 10, .usage = "set-hook [-agpRuw] [-t target-pane] hook [command]", .handler = cmdSetHook'),
    ('show-hooks", .alias = null, .min_args = 0, .max_args = 6, .usage = "show-hooks [-gpw] [-t target-pane] [hook]", .handler = cmdNotImplemented',
     'show-hooks", .alias = null, .min_args = 0, .max_args = 6, .usage = "show-hooks [-gpw] [-t target-pane] [hook]", .handler = cmdShowHooks'),
    ('.handler = cmdNotImplemented });\n\n        // -- Display commands',
     '.handler = cmdNotImplemented });\n\n        // -- Display commands'),  # no-op, just to verify structure
    ('"display-menu (menu) [-MO] [-b border-lines] [-c target-client] [-C starting-choice] [-H selected-style] [-s style] [-S border-style] [-t target-pane] [-T title] [-x position] [-y position] name [key] [command] ...", .handler = cmdNotImplemented',
     '"display-menu (menu) [-MO] [-b border-lines] [-c target-client] [-C starting-choice] [-H selected-style] [-s style] [-S border-style] [-t target-pane] [-T title] [-x position] [-y position] name [key] [command] ...", .handler = cmdDisplayMenu'),
    ('"display-popup (popup) [-BCEkN] [-b border-lines] [-c target-client] [-d start-directory] [-e environment] [-h height] [-s style] [-S border-style] [-t target-pane] [-T title] [-w width] [-x position] [-y position] [shell-command [argument ...]]", .handler = cmdNotImplemented',
     '"display-popup (popup) [-BCEkN] [-b border-lines] [-c target-client] [-d start-directory] [-e environment] [-h height] [-s style] [-S border-style] [-t target-pane] [-T title] [-w width] [-x position] [-y position] [shell-command [argument ...]]", .handler = cmdDisplayPopup'),
    ('"choose-client [-NrZ] [-F format] [-f filter] [-K key-format] [-O sort-order] [-t target-pane] [template]", .handler = cmdNotImplemented',
     '"choose-client [-NrZ] [-F format] [-f filter] [-K key-format] [-O sort-order] [-t target-pane] [template]", .handler = cmdChooseClient'),
    ('"confirm-before (confirm) [-by] [-c confirm-key] [-p prompt] [-t target-client] command", .handler = cmdNotImplemented',
     '"confirm-before (confirm) [-by] [-c confirm-key] [-p prompt] [-t target-client] command", .handler = cmdConfirmBefore'),
    ('"customize-mode [-NZ] [-F format] [-f filter] [-t target-pane]", .handler = cmdNotImplemented',
     '"customize-mode [-NZ] [-F format] [-f filter] [-t target-pane]", .handler = cmdCustomizeMode'),
    ('"wait-for (wait) [-L|-S|-U] channel", .handler = cmdNotImplemented',
     '"wait-for (wait) [-L|-S|-U] channel", .handler = cmdWaitFor'),
    ('"clear-prompt-history (clearphist) [-T prompt-type]", .handler = cmdNotImplemented',
     '"clear-prompt-history (clearphist) [-T prompt-type]", .handler = cmdClearPromptHistory'),
    ('"show-prompt-history (showphist) [-T prompt-type]", .handler = cmdNotImplemented',
     '"show-prompt-history (showphist) [-T prompt-type]", .handler = cmdShowPromptHistory'),
    ('"server-access [-adlrw] [-t target-pane] [user]", .handler = cmdNotImplemented',
     '"server-access [-adlrw] [-t target-pane] [user]", .handler = cmdServerAccess'),
]

changed = 0
for old, new in replacements:
    if old in src:
        src = src.replace(old, new, 1)
        changed += 1
    else:
        print(f"NOT FOUND: {old[:60]!r}")

with open(path, 'w') as f:
    f.write(src)

print(f"Applied {changed}/{len(replacements)} replacements")
PYEOF

Task 5 complete. All display, config, shell, hook, option, environment, and access commands implemented. Build and full test suite pass (`zig build test` → exit 0).

**Files changed:**
- `src/core/environ.zig`: Added `setHidden()` method
- `src/server.zig`: Added `hooks_mod` import, `AclEntry` struct, and four new fields (`hook_registry`, `wait_channels`, `prompt_history`, `acl_entries`) with init/deinit
- `src/cmd/cmd.zig`:
  - Added `hooks_mod` import
  - **New commands**: `cmdChooseClient`, `cmdConfirmBefore`, `cmdCustomizeMode`, `cmdDisplayMenu`, `cmdDisplayPopup`, `cmdSetHook`, `cmdShowHooks`, `cmdClearPromptHistory`, `cmdShowPromptHistory`, `cmdWaitFor`, `cmdServerAccess`
  - **Hook helpers**: `hookTypeFromName()`, `hookTypeName()`
  - **Expanded flags**: `cmdClockMode` (-t), `cmdDisplayMessage` (-a/-C/-I/-l/-N/-p/-v/-c/-d/-F/-t), `cmdCommandPrompt` (-1/-b/-e/-F/-i/-k/-l/-N/-I/-p/-t/-T), `cmdCopyMode` (-d/-e/-H/-M/-q/-S/-u/-s/-t), `cmdSourceFile` (-F/-n/-q/-v/-t), `cmdIfShell` (-b/-F), `cmdRunShell` (-b/-C/-E/-c/-d/-t), `cmdSetOption` (-a/-F/-o/-p/-q/-s/-u/-U/-w), `cmdShowOptions` (-A/-g/-H/-p/-q/-s/-v/-w)
  - **Full implementations**: `cmdSetEnvironment`, `cmdShowEnvironment`
  - **Extended**: `applySessionOption` with `visual-activity`, `status-left`, `status-right`
  - All `registerBuiltins` entries wired to new handlers
- `src/cmd/display_commands_test.zig`: 8 tests (new)
- `src/cmd/option_commands_test.zig`: 9 tests (new)
- `src/cmd/config_shell_test.zig`: 15 tests (new)
- `src/main.zig`: Registered all three test files
